name: Porter v. McCollum
question: Did the Eleventh Circuit err when it held that a person's Sixth Amendment right to effective counsel was not violated when his attorney at sentencing failed to bring forward evidence of his heroic war record and other mitigating evidence.
description: A case in which the Court held that an attorney's failure to bring forth evidence of a client's heroic war record and other mitigating evidence violated the client's Sixth Amendment rights to effective counsel.
